binaries.title: ndis.sys - WIL feature-staging graduation moving \Device\Ndis per-handle context free from IRP_MJ_CLEANUP to IRP_MJ_CLOSE

changed_functions.name: ndisDispatchRequest
key_changes: IRP_MJ_CLEANUP (MajorFunction==0x12) handler: removed the Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() gate (call at 0x1C0002CF5) and the ExFreePoolWithTag block it guarded. The 'NDoC' pool is no longer freed during CLEANUP; the handler performs miniport/list teardown and completes the IRP.

IRP_MJ_CLOSE (MajorFunction==2) handler: removed the Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() gate (call at 0x1C0002D45). The pool is now freed unconditionally (NULL check retained) during CLOSE.

Both Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() calls are deleted; feature ID 3003324730 is fully retired and its two helper functions (Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage @ 0x1C00373E8 and Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledFallback @ 0x1C0037420) are removed from the patched binary.
The 'NDoC' allocation counter shifted from dword_1C00F5C28 (unpatched) to dword_1C00F5C20 (patched) due to a data-section relocation, not a logic change.
changed_functions.security_notes: The correct name of the dispatcher is ndisDispatchRequest (@0x1C0002B08), reached from ndisDriverDispatch (@0x1C0002AE0) when the DeviceObject is not ndisLwmDeviceObject. The function labelled sub_1c00373e8 in the original draft is Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (@0x1C00373E8), a WIL feature-staging enablement check, not a hand-rolled global-flag gate. In the unpatched build the gate selects WHERE the 0x38-byte per-handle context pool (tag 'NDoC' = 0x636F444E) is freed: feature disabled -> free in IRP_MJ_CLEANUP; feature enabled -> free in IRP_MJ_CLOSE. The two paths are complementary (exactly one frees), not an inverted double-free. The patch removes the gate and commits to freeing the pool only in IRP_MJ_CLOSE. This is the graduation of a staged object-lifetime change (deferring the free to the later teardown callback). No reachable use-after-free is demonstrable: the pool pointer is held only in FileObject->FsContext (offset +0x18), which is set to NULL when freed; the miniport at pool+0x20 is referenced (ndisReferenceMiniportByHandle) and released (ndisDereferenceMiniport) within the CLEANUP block; the LIST_ENTRY at pool+0x8/+0x10 is unlinked under the AoAc spinlock before the free; and the only other user-reachable major function, IRP_MJ_DEVICE_CONTROL (0xE), routes to ndisHandlePnPRequest and never dereferences FsContext. The change is a lifetime refactor delivered through WIL feature staging, not a security fix.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED ndisDispatchRequest (key sections):

; IRP_MJ_CLOSE (MajorFunction==2) path - feature-gated free:
0x1C0002D45: call    Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1C0002D4A: test    eax, eax
0x1C0002D4C: jz      loc_1C0002E03          ; feature disabled -> skip free
0x1C0002D52: mov     rax, [r15+30h]         ; FileObject
0x1C0002D56: mov     rcx, [rax+18h]         ; FsContext (pool)
0x1C0002D5A: mov     [rax+18h], rbx         ; FsContext = NULL
0x1C0002D5E: test    rcx, rcx
0x1C0002D61: jz      short loc_1C0002D1B
0x1C0002D63: jmp     short loc_1C0002D0D    ; -> shared free/dec block
0x1C0002D0D: xor     edx, edx
0x1C0002D0F: call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag
0x1C0002D1B: lock dec cs:dword_1C00F5C28

; IRP_MJ_CLEANUP (MajorFunction==0x12) path - teardown then feature-gated free:
0x1C0002CF5: call    Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1C0002CFA: test    eax, eax
0x1C0002CFC: jnz     loc_1C0002E03          ; feature enabled -> skip free
0x1C0002D02: mov     rax, [r15+30h]
0x1C0002D06: mov     rcx, rdi              ; pool pointer
0x1C0002D09: mov     [rax+18h], rbx        ; FsContext = NULL
0x1C0002D0D: xor     edx, edx
0x1C0002D0F: call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag
0x1C0002D1B: lock dec cs:dword_1C00F5C28

PATCHED ndisDispatchRequest (key sections):

; IRP_MJ_CLOSE (MajorFunction==2) path - unconditional free (no feature gate):
0x1C0002D21: mov     rax, [rdi+30h]         ; FileObject
0x1C0002D25: mov     rcx, [rax+18h]         ; FsContext (pool)
0x1C0002D29: mov     [rax+18h], rbx         ; FsContext = NULL
0x1C0002D2D: test    rcx, rcx               ; NULL check only
0x1C0002D30: jz      short loc_1C0002D40
0x1C0002D32: xor     edx, edx
0x1C0002D34: call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag
0x1C0002D40: lock dec cs:dword_1C00F5C20

; IRP_MJ_CLEANUP (MajorFunction==0x12) path - no free:
0x1C0002CE7: mov     rcx, [rdi+30h]
0x1C0002CEB: test    rcx, rcx
0x1C0002CEE: jz      loc_1C0002DEA
0x1C0002CF4: call    ?ndisIfDereferenceCompartmentForUser@@YAJPEAX@Z
0x1C0002CF9: mov     [rdi+30h], rbx
0x1C0002CFD: jmp     loc_1C0002DEA          ; -> completion, no ExFreePoolWithTag

security_findings.entry_point: CreateFile("\\.\\NDIS") / CloseHandle - IRP_MJ_CREATE, IRP_MJ_CLEANUP and IRP_MJ_CLOSE dispatched via \Device\Ndis through ndisDriverDispatch (@0x1C0002AE0) -> ndisDispatchRequest
call_chain: User-mode: CreateFile("\\.\\NDIS") opens the handle (IRP_MJ_CREATE); CloseHandle() produces IRP_MJ_CLEANUP then IRP_MJ_CLOSE
ndisDriverDispatch (@0x1C0002AE0) - routes to ndisDispatchRequest when DeviceObject != ndisLwmDeviceObject, else ndisLwmDispatchIrp
ndisDispatchRequest (@0x1C0002B08) - allocates the 'NDoC' pool on CREATE; on CLEANUP/CLOSE the (unpatched) Feature_3003324730 gate selects which callback frees it
security_findings.vulnerability_class: None - WIL feature-staging graduation / object-lifetime refactor (no reachable memory-safety issue)
security_findings.description: During IRP_MJ_CREATE (MajorFunction==0), ndisDispatchRequest allocates a 0x38-byte NonPagedPool object (tag 'NDoC' = 0x636F444E) via ExAllocatePool2(POOL_FLAG_NON_PAGED, 0x38, ...) and stores it in CurrentStackLocation->FileObject->FsContext (FileObject+0x18). The structure holds a LIST_ENTRY at +0x8/+0x10, counters at +0x18/+0x1c, a miniport-block pointer at +0x20, and a compartment handle at +0x30. In the unpatched build, both the IRP_MJ_CLEANUP (0x12) and IRP_MJ_CLOSE (2) paths call Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (@0x1C00373E8), a WIL feature-enablement check that reads Feature_3003324730__private_featureState (bit 0x10 cached, bit 0x1 enabled) and otherwise consults the descriptor via wil_details_IsEnabledFallback. The gate selects the free site: feature disabled -> free in CLEANUP, feature enabled -> free in CLOSE. The two paths are complementary, so exactly one of them frees the pool; there is no inverted double-free. The patch deletes both gate calls, removes the free from CLEANUP entirely, and makes CLOSE free the pool unconditionally (NULL check retained); the two now-unused Feature_3003324730 helper functions are dropped. No reachable use-after-free is demonstrable in either build: the pool pointer is held only in FileObject->FsContext, which is NULLed on free; the miniport reference taken during CLEANUP teardown is released in the same block; the LIST_ENTRY is unlinked under the AoAc spinlock before any free; and IRP_MJ_DEVICE_CONTROL (0xE) routes to ndisHandlePnPRequest and never dereferences FsContext. Deferring the free from CLEANUP to CLOSE is a lifetime-correctness refactor delivered through WIL feature staging, not a memory-safety fix.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. CreateFile("\\.\\NDIS", ...) triggers IRP_MJ_CREATE, allocating the 0x38-byte 'NDoC' pool into FileObject->FsContext. 2. CloseHandle() produces IRP_MJ_CLEANUP (0x12) then IRP_MJ_CLOSE (2). In the unpatched build the Feature_3003324730 gate decides which callback frees the pool; in the patched build the free always occurs in IRP_MJ_CLOSE. No cross-callback stale reference is reachable: the freed pointer lives only in FsContext (NULLed on free), and no user-reachable path dereferences the pool after it is freed.
security_findings.primitive: None. No exploit primitive is present. The freed pool pointer is confined to FileObject->FsContext and is set to NULL at the point of free; no surviving live pointer to the freed allocation exists on any user-reachable path. There is no demonstrable use-after-free, no arbitrary read/write, and no privilege-escalation primitive.
security_findings.assembly:
ndisDispatchRequest (UNPATCHED, ndis_unpatched.sys) - relevant regions:

; --- MajorFunction dispatch ---
0x1C0002B45: movzx   r8d, byte ptr [r15]              ; MajorFunction
0x1C0002B4C: jz      loc_1C0002D65                    ; r8==0  -> IRP_MJ_CREATE
0x1C0002B52: sub     r8d, 2
0x1C0002B56: jz      loc_1C0002D45                    ; r8==2  -> IRP_MJ_CLOSE
0x1C0002B5C: sub     r8d, 0Ch
0x1C0002B60: jz      loc_1C0002D2E                    ; r8==0xE -> DEVICE_CONTROL (ndisHandlePnPRequest)
0x1C0002B70: sub     r8d, 3
0x1C0002B74: jz      short loc_1C0002B96             ; r8==0x12 -> IRP_MJ_CLEANUP

; --- IRP_MJ_CREATE (r8==0) ---
0x1C0002D65: mov     edx, 38h                        ; size 0x38
0x1C0002D6A: mov     r8d, 636F444Eh                  ; tag 'NDoC'
0x1C0002D70: lea     ecx, [rdx+8]                    ; POOL_FLAG_NON_PAGED
0x1C0002D73: call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePool2
0x1C0002DEE: mov     [rax+18h], rdi                  ; FileObject->FsContext = pool
0x1C0002DF2: lock inc cs:dword_1C00F5C28

; --- IRP_MJ_CLOSE (r8==2) - feature-gated free ---
0x1C0002D45: call    Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1C0002D4A: test    eax, eax
0x1C0002D4C: jz      loc_1C0002E03                    ; feature disabled -> skip free
0x1C0002D52: mov     rax, [r15+30h]
0x1C0002D56: mov     rcx, [rax+18h]
0x1C0002D5A: mov     [rax+18h], rbx
0x1C0002D5E: test    rcx, rcx
0x1C0002D61: jz      short loc_1C0002D1B
0x1C0002D63: jmp     short loc_1C0002D0D

; --- IRP_MJ_CLEANUP (r8==0x12) - teardown then feature-gated free ---
0x1C0002B96: mov     rax, [r15+30h]                   ; FileObject
0x1C0002B9A: mov     rdi, [rax+18h]                   ; pool pointer
0x1C0002B9E: mov     rsi, [rdi+20h]                   ; miniport block (pool+0x20)
; ... ndisReferenceMiniportByHandle / KeAcquireSpinLockRaiseToDpc / LIST_ENTRY unlink /
;     ndisDereferenceMiniport - all balanced within this block ...
0x1C0002CE3: mov     rcx, [rdi+30h]                   ; compartment handle (pool+0x30)
0x1C0002CEC: call    ?ndisIfDereferenceCompartmentForUser@@YAJPEAX@Z
0x1C0002CF1: mov     [rdi+30h], rbx
0x1C0002CF5: call    Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1C0002CFA: test    eax, eax
0x1C0002CFC: jnz     loc_1C0002E03                    ; feature enabled -> skip free
0x1C0002D02: mov     rax, [r15+30h]
0x1C0002D06: mov     rcx, rdi
0x1C0002D09: mov     [rax+18h], rbx
0x1C0002D0D: xor     edx, edx
0x1C0002D0F: call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag
0x1C0002D1B: lock dec cs:dword_1C00F5C28
0x1C0002D22: jmp     loc_1C0002E03

; --- completion / epilogue ---
0x1C0002E03: mov     dl, 2
0x1C0002E05: mov     [rbp+30h], ebx
0x1C0002E0B: call    cs:__imp_IofCompleteRequest
0x1C0002E1E: call    cs:__imp_MmUnlockPagableImageSection
0x1C0002E2A: lock dec cs:?ndisPkgs@@3PAU_PKG_REF@@A

PATCHED ndisDispatchRequest - changed regions:

; --- IRP_MJ_CLOSE (r8==2) - unconditional free ---
0x1C0002D21: mov     rax, [rdi+30h]
0x1C0002D25: mov     rcx, [rax+18h]
0x1C0002D29: mov     [rax+18h], rbx
0x1C0002D2D: test    rcx, rcx
0x1C0002D30: jz      short loc_1C0002D40
0x1C0002D32: xor     edx, edx
0x1C0002D34: call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag
0x1C0002D40: lock dec cs:dword_1C00F5C20

; --- IRP_MJ_CLEANUP (r8==0x12) - no free ---
0x1C0002CE7: mov     rcx, [rdi+30h]
0x1C0002CEB: test    rcx, rcx
0x1C0002CEE: jz      loc_1C0002DEA
0x1C0002CF4: call    ?ndisIfDereferenceCompartmentForUser@@YAJPEAX@Z
0x1C0002CF9: mov     [rdi+30h], rbx
0x1C0002CFD: jmp     loc_1C0002DEA

breakpoints.why: Dispatcher for \Device\Ndis (reached from ndisDriverDispatch when DeviceObject != ndisLwmDeviceObject). Break here to inspect the MajorFunction; r15 = IO_STACK_LOCATION (loaded from IRP+0xB8); r8b = MajorFunction after 0x1C0002B45 (0=CREATE, 2=CLOSE, 0xE=DEVICE_CONTROL, 0x12=CLEANUP).

WIL feature-enablement check (unpatched only). Reads Feature_3003324730__private_featureState; return 0 -> 'NDoC' pool freed in IRP_MJ_CLEANUP; return non-zero -> freed in IRP_MJ_CLOSE. Absent from the patched binary.
Patched IRP_MJ_CLOSE unconditional free path: rcx = FsContext pool pointer, edx=0 (tag). In the patched build this is the only site that frees the 'NDoC' pool.
Top-level dispatch: rcx = DeviceObject, rdx = IRP. cmp rcx, ndisLwmDeviceObject determines routing to ndisDispatchRequest vs ndisLwmDispatchIrp.

watch: eax after Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (unpatched 0x1C0002CFA / 0x1C0002D4A) - selects which callback frees the pool
rcx at ExFreePoolWithTag - pool pointer being freed (tag 'NDoC', 0x38 bytes)
Feature_3003324730__private_featureState - WIL feature state (bit 0x10 cached, bit 0x1 enabled)
key_offsets: 0x1C0002B45: movzx r8d, byte ptr [r15] - reads MajorFunction
0x1C0002D0F: call ExFreePoolWithTag - CLEANUP free site (removed in patch)
0x1C0002D45: call Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage - CLOSE gate (removed in patch, free made unconditional)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. HANDLE h = CreateFileW(L"\\.\\NDIS", GENERIC_READ|GENERIC_WRITE, 0, NULL, OPEN_EXISTING, 0, NULL); triggers IRP_MJ_CREATE (r8==0), allocating the 0x38-byte 'NDoC' pool into FileObject->FsContext. 2. CloseHandle(h); produces IRP_MJ_CLEANUP (0x12) then IRP_MJ_CLOSE (2). Unpatched: the Feature_3003324730 gate selects the free site. Patched: the pool is always freed in IRP_MJ_CLOSE and never in IRP_MJ_CLEANUP. No cross-callback stale reference is reachable.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Unpatched: at Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage the return value selects the free callback (0 -> CLEANUP frees; non-zero -> CLOSE frees). Exactly one callback frees the pool, and FileObject->FsContext is NULLed at that point. Patched: IRP_MJ_CLEANUP performs teardown and completes without freeing; IRP_MJ_CLOSE (0x1C0002D21) frees the pool. No use-after-free is observable in either build.
debugging_aids.notes: The pool tag is 0x636F444E = 'NDoC' (little-endian bytes 'NDoC'). Pool size 0x38 bytes, POOL_FLAG_NON_PAGED (0x40). Layout: +0x00 access-check bytes (3 used), +0x08 LIST_ENTRY.Flink, +0x10 LIST_ENTRY.Blink, +0x18 counter/state, +0x1c counter, +0x20 miniport block pointer, +0x28 reserved, +0x30 compartment handle. Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage reads Feature_3003324730__private_featureState: if bit 0x10 is set it returns bit 0x1; otherwise it calls Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledFallback -> wil_details_IsEnabledFallback on Feature_3003324730__private_descriptor. The patch retires feature ID 3003324730 entirely (both helper functions removed). This is a WIL feature-staging graduation of an object-lifetime refactor, not a security fix.

summary: The only code change between ndis_unpatched.sys and ndis_patched.sys is the retirement of a WIL feature-staging gate (Feature_3003324730__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage) inside ndisDispatchRequest (@0x1C0002B08), the IRP dispatcher for the \Device\Ndis control device. In the unpatched build the gate selects where the 0x38-byte per-handle context pool (tag 'NDoC') is freed: feature disabled frees it in IRP_MJ_CLEANUP, feature enabled frees it in IRP_MJ_CLOSE; the two paths are complementary, so exactly one frees the pool. The patch removes the gate and its two helper functions and commits to freeing the pool only in IRP_MJ_CLOSE. This is the graduation of a staged object-lifetime change (deferring the free to the later teardown callback). No reachable use-after-free is demonstrable: the pool pointer is held only in FileObject->FsContext, which is NULLed on free; the miniport reference taken during CLEANUP teardown is released in the same block; and IRP_MJ_DEVICE_CONTROL does not dereference FsContext. An independent whole-binary comparison confirmed ndisDispatchRequest is the sole functional change (all other flagged functions differ only in relocated data-symbol/label addresses). The original draft's premature-pool-free use-after-free, its inverted-condition framing, and its exploit/PoC chain are not supported by the binaries. Verdict: no security-relevant change.
